couplets.label: K1
couplets.question: Upperside forewing vein 4 not or only very slightly swollen in ♂; veins 6 and 7 separate (except in A. agnolia). Subgenus Allotinus.
couplets.a_text: Upperside forewing vein 4 not or only very slightly swollen in ♂; veins 6 and 7 separate (except in A. agnolia). → Subgenus Allotinus
couplets.b_text: ♂ forewing vein 4 swollen; veins 6 and 7 connate or stalked (except sometimes in the nivalis group of Paragerydus). → Subgenus Fabitaras or Paragerydus
couplets.hint: Look at the ♂ forewing: in Subgenus Allotinus, vein 4 is slender and veins 6 and 7 arise separately from the discal cell. In Fabitaras/Paragerydus, vein 4 is conspicuously thickened at the base.

couplets.label: K2
couplets.question: Upperside with pale blue or white areas.
couplets.a_text: Upperside with pale blue or white areas.
couplets.b_text: Upperside brown. ♂ forewing vein 4 weakly swollen for one-quarter its length. Sumatra and Borneo. → Allotinus nicholsi
couplets.hint: Within Subgenus Allotinus four species show pale blue or white on the upperside; only A. nicholsi is entirely brown above.

couplets.label: K3
couplets.question: Upperside with pale blue areas.
couplets.a_text: Upperside with pale blue areas. → Allotinus subviolaceus
couplets.b_text: Upperside with white areas.
couplets.hint: A. subviolaceus is the only Allotinus with a blue-sheen upperside. All remaining species here show white rather than blue.

couplets.label: K4
couplets.question: Upperside hindwing with a broad white band.
couplets.a_text: Upperside hindwing with a broad white band. → Allotinus albifasciatus
couplets.b_text: Upperside hindwing brown.
couplets.hint: A. albifasciatus has a distinctive broad white band across the hindwing upperside, absent in A. fallax and A. agnolia.

couplets.label: K5
couplets.question: Forewing veins 6 and 7 separate; vein 4 not swollen in ♂.
couplets.a_text: Forewing veins 6 and 7 separate; vein 4 not swollen in ♂. → Allotinus fallax
couplets.b_text: Forewing veins 6 and 7 stalked; vein 4 weakly swollen in ♂. Sumatra. → Allotinus agnolia
couplets.hint: Both species have a white forewing with a brown margin. Separate by examining the fusion state of forewing veins 6 and 7.

couplets.label: K6
couplets.question: Hindwing weak precostal vein normally present; ♂ abdominal hair tufts inconspicuous. Subgenus Fabitaras.
couplets.a_text: Hindwing weak precostal vein normally present. ♂ valva with apical process; abdominal hair tufts inconspicuous. → Subgenus Fabitaras
couplets.b_text: Hindwing without precostal vein. ♂ valva with costa abruptly truncate ending in a ventral pointed process; abdominal hair tufts prominent and permanently extruded. → Subgenus Paragerydus
couplets.hint: This distinction requires close examination. In the field, Fabitaras species tend to be smaller with a rounded hindwing (except the two tailed species); Paragerydus species are more variable in size and pattern.

couplets.label: K7
couplets.question: Underside hindwing postdiscal series with spot in space 6 placed below or just inside spot in space 7; ♂ hindwing caudate (tailed) at vein 4.
couplets.a_text: Underside hindwing postdiscal series with spot in space 6 placed below or just inside the spot in space 7. ♂ hindwing caudate at vein 4 (tailed).
couplets.b_text: Underside hindwing postdiscal stria in space 6 placed well outside the stria in space 7. ♂ hindwing rounded (tailless).
couplets.hint: Check for a short tail (finger-like projection) at the hindwing vein 4 tip. The position of the postdiscal spot in space 6 relative to space 7 provides a corroborating underside character.

couplets.label: K8
couplets.question: Underside postdiscal spots enlarged and blotchy above vein 4 on forewing and vein 6 on hindwing; striations less dense on a greyish white ground.
couplets.a_text: Underside postdiscal spots enlarged and blotchy above vein 4 on forewing and vein 6 on hindwing; other striations less dense on a greyish white ground. → Allotinus fabius
couplets.b_text: Underside postdiscal series rather inconspicuous; forewing apex shaded with reddish brown, other striations dense on a buff ground. → Allotinus borneensis
couplets.hint: Both species are tailed. A. fabius has distinctive enlarged blotchy postdiscal spots on a grey-white ground. A. borneensis has a reddish-brown forewing apex and denser striations on a buff ground.

couplets.label: K9
couplets.question: Upperside hindwing entirely brown (not white in tornal quarter).
couplets.a_text: Upperside hindwing brown.
couplets.b_text: Upperside hindwing tornal quarter white. Borneo. → Allotinus brooksi
couplets.hint: A. brooksi (Borneo) is the only tailless Fabitaras with a white tornal patch on the hindwing upperside. All other tailless Fabitaras have a uniformly brown hindwing.

couplets.label: K10
couplets.question: Underside hindwing postdiscal stria in space 6 on a more or less even arc with the striae in spaces 2–5; ♂ forewing vein 4 swollen for well over half its length.
couplets.a_text: Underside hindwing postdiscal stria in space 6 placed on a more or less even arc with the striae in spaces 2–5. ♂ forewing vein 4 swollen for well over half its length.
couplets.b_text: Underside hindwing postdiscal stria in space 6 placed mid-way between the striae in spaces 5 and 7. ♂ forewing vein 4 swollen for half its length or less.
couplets.hint: In A. sarrastes and A. portunus, the hindwing postdiscal spot in space 6 lines up in a smooth arc with spaces 2–5. In A. strigatus and A. bidiensis it sits mid-way between spaces 5 and 7.

couplets.label: K11
couplets.question: Underside forewing postdiscal series dislocated at vein 4 and closer to the termen at the dorsum than at the costa; submarginal blackish spots outwardly edged with white.
couplets.a_text: Underside forewing postdiscal series dislocated at vein 4 and closer to the termen at the dorsum than at the costa; submarginal blackish spots outwardly edged with white. → Allotinus sarrastes
couplets.b_text: Underside forewing postdiscal series parallel to termen; submarginal spots not white edged in ♂, but weakly so in ♀. → Allotinus portunus
couplets.hint: Examine the forewing postdiscal row at vein 4: in A. sarrastes the spots on the dorsal side are displaced toward the wing margin (dislocation). Also check for white edging on the submarginal spots.

couplets.label: K12
couplets.question: Underside pale buff; ♂ forewing vein 4 swollen for half its length; visual brand about 2.0 mm wide.
couplets.a_text: Underside pale buff. ♂ forewing vein 4 swollen for half its length; visual brand 2.0 mm wide. → Allotinus strigatus
couplets.b_text: Underside white. ♂ forewing vein 4 swollen for one third its length; visual brand inconspicuous and less than 1.0 mm wide. Borneo. → Allotinus bidiensis
couplets.hint: Compare the underside ground colour (pale buff vs white) and the size of the ♂ visual brand on vein 4 (2.0 mm vs less than 1.0 mm).

couplets.label: K13
couplets.question: Upperside brown (without pale blue patch on hindwing in ♂).
couplets.a_text: Upperside brown.
couplets.b_text: Underside greyish white or white. ♂ upperside with broad brown borders; hindwing with a pale shining blue patch. ♀ upperside forewing with a white discal patch and blue scaling at the base. Sumatra, Borneo, Palawan, Philippines. → Allotinus melos
couplets.hint: A. melos stands out by the male's conspicuous blue patch on the hindwing upperside. All remaining Paragerydus species are uniformly brown above.

couplets.label: K14
couplets.question: Forewing with a white costal fleck at the end of vein 7; ♂ forewing without a visual brand.
couplets.a_text: Forewing with a white costal fleck at the end of vein 7. ♂ forewing without a visual brand.
couplets.b_text: Forewing without a white costal fleck at the end of vein 7. ♂ forewing with a visual brand (except in A. corbeti).
couplets.hint: Look for a small pale or white spot on the forewing costa near the apex (end of vein 7). This feature splits the brown Paragerydus into two groups.

couplets.label: K15
couplets.question: Underside hindwing central spot in space 7 blackened; smaller (forewing length 9.0–14.0 mm).
couplets.a_text: Underside hindwing central spot in space 7 blackened. Smaller, forewing length 9.0–14.0 mm.
couplets.b_text: Underside hindwing central spot in space 7 not blackened. Larger, forewing length 12.5–15.5 mm. Borneo. → Allotinus nivalis
couplets.hint: Look for the central postdiscal spot in hindwing space 7. In A. substrigosus and A. davidis this spot is darkened; in the larger A. nivalis (Borneo) it is not.

couplets.label: K16
couplets.question: Underside greyish white; hindwing postdiscal spot in space 7 not blackened on its inner edge.
couplets.a_text: Underside greyish white; hindwing postdiscal spot in space 7 not blackened on its inner edge. → Allotinus substrigosus
couplets.b_text: Underside pale buff; hindwing postdiscal spot in space 7 blackened on its inner edge. → Allotinus davidis
couplets.hint: Compare the underside ground colour (grey-white vs pale buff) and whether the inner edge of the space 7 postdiscal spot is darkened.

couplets.label: K17
couplets.question: Small (forewing length 10.0–12.0 mm); ♂ forewing vein 4 swollen for only one third its length; no visual brand; upperside uniform reddish brown.
couplets.a_text: Small, forewing length 10.0–12.0 mm. ♂ forewing apex rounded, vein 4 swollen for one third its length, no visual brand. ♂♀ upperside uniform reddish brown; underside marked like A. leogoron. → Allotinus corbeti
couplets.b_text: Larger. ♂ forewing more or less produced; vein 4 swollen for at least half its length, visual brand present.
couplets.hint: A. corbeti is the smallest Paragerydus without a visual brand. Its uniform reddish-brown upperside and small size are distinctive.

couplets.label: K18
couplets.question: Underside forewing postdiscal series parallel to termen and usually rather obscure; hindwing not dentate; ♂ forewing brand oval and broad, extending more than half length of vein 4.
couplets.a_text: Underside forewing postdiscal series parallel to termen and usually rather obscure. Hindwing not dentate. ♂ upperside forewing brand oval, broad, extending more than half length of vein 4. ♀ upperside forewing with a paler area beyond end cell. → Allotinus apries
couplets.b_text: Underside forewing postdiscal series nearer termen at dorsum than at costa. Hindwing dentate, weakly so in ♂, more strongly in ♀.
couplets.hint: A. apries has a broad oval brand and a smooth (non-dentate) hindwing margin. The remaining species have a kinked postdiscal row and a dentate hindwing outer margin.

couplets.label: K19
couplets.question: Underside forewing postdiscal series dislocated at veins 4 and 2; hindwing postdiscal spot in space 6 placed below or just inside spot in space 7; ♂ forewing brand more than half length of vein 4.
couplets.a_text: Underside forewing postdiscal series dislocated at veins 4 and 2; hindwing with postdiscal spot in space 6 placed below or just inside the outer spot in space 7. ♂ upperside forewing brand more than half length of vein 4.
couplets.b_text: Underside postdiscal series not conspicuously dislocated; ♂ forewing without a visual brand. The most common and widespread species of the genus. → Allotinus unicolor
couplets.hint: A. unicolor is the most common Malaysian Allotinus, recognised by its plain dark brown wings and absence of a visual brand in the male. A. horsfieldi and A. leogoron both have prominent brands and dislocated postdiscal rows.

couplets.label: K20
couplets.question: ♂ forewing brand comparatively narrow, touching vein 3 only at its base; uncus tip rostriform; underside ground colour pale buff.
couplets.a_text: ♂ upperside forewing brand comparatively narrow, touching vein 3 only at its base. Uncus tip rostriform. Underside ground colour pale buff. → Allotinus horsfieldi
couplets.b_text: ♂ upperside forewing brand broad, touching vein 3 almost throughout its length. Uncus tip blunt. Underside ground colour greyish white, striations darker brown. ♀ hindwing dentations narrower than in A. horsfieldi. → Allotinus leogoron
couplets.hint: These two similar species are best separated by brand width (narrow vs broad), uncus shape, and underside colour (buff vs grey-white).

leads.3: Upperside with pale blue areas. → Allotinus subviolaceus
leads.5: Upperside hindwing with a broad white band. → Allotinus albifasciatus
leads.7: Forewing veins 6 and 7 separate; vein 4 not swollen in ♂. → Allotinus fallax
leads.8: Forewing veins 6 and 7 stalked; vein 4 weakly swollen in ♂. Sumatra. → Allotinus agnolia
leads.9: Upperside brown. ♂ forewing vein 4 weakly swollen for one-quarter its length. Sumatra and Borneo. → Allotinus nicholsi
leads.13: Underside postdiscal spots enlarged and blotchy above vein 4 on forewing and vein 6 on hindwing; other striations less dense on a greyish white ground. → Allotinus fabius
leads.14: Underside postdiscal series rather inconspicuous; forewing apex shaded with reddish brown, other striations dense on a buff ground. → Allotinus borneensis
leads.18: Underside forewing postdiscal series dislocated at vein 4 and closer to the termen at the dorsum than at the costa; submarginal blackish spots outwardly edged with white. → Allotinus sarrastes
leads.19: Underside forewing postdiscal series parallel to termen; submarginal spots not white edged in ♂, but weakly so in ♀. → Allotinus portunus
leads.21: Underside pale buff. ♂ forewing vein 4 swollen for half its length; visual brand 2.0 mm wide. → Allotinus strigatus
leads.22: Underside white. ♂ forewing vein 4 swollen for one third its length; visual brand inconspicuous and less than 1.0 mm wide. Borneo. → Allotinus bidiensis
leads.23: Upperside hindwing tornal quarter white. Borneo. → Allotinus brooksi
leads.28: Underside greyish white; hindwing postdiscal spot in space 7 not blackened on its inner edge. → Allotinus substrigosus
leads.29: Underside pale buff; hindwing postdiscal spot in space 7 blackened on its inner edge. → Allotinus davidis
leads.30: Underside hindwing central spot in space 7 not blackened. Larger, forewing length 12.5–15.5 mm. Borneo. → Allotinus nivalis
leads.32: Small, forewing length 10.0–12.0 mm. ♂ forewing apex rounded, vein 4 swollen for one third its length, no visual brand. ♂♀ upperside uniform reddish brown; underside marked like A. leogoron. → Allotinus corbeti
leads.34: Underside forewing postdiscal series parallel to termen and usually rather obscure. Hindwing not dentate. ♂ upperside forewing brand oval, broad, extending more than half length of vein 4. ♀ upperside forewing with a paler area beyond end cell. → Allotinus apries
leads.37: ♂ upperside forewing brand comparatively narrow, touching vein 3 only at its base. Uncus tip rostriform. Underside ground colour pale buff. → Allotinus horsfieldi
leads.38: ♂ upperside forewing brand broad, touching vein 3 almost throughout its length. Uncus tip blunt. Underside ground colour greyish white, striations darker brown. ♀ hindwing dentations narrower than in A. horsfieldi. → Allotinus leogoron
leads.39: Underside postdiscal series not conspicuously dislocated; ♂ forewing without a visual brand. The most common and widespread species of the genus. → Allotinus unicolor
leads.40: Underside greyish white or white. ♂ upperside with broad brown borders; hindwing with a pale shining blue patch. ♀ upperside forewing with a white discal patch and blue scaling at the base. Sumatra, Borneo, Palawan, Philippines. → Allotinus melos
